digest.top_line: mRCAT-III: pCR 61% vs 28.6% (P<0.0001) adding node-sparing SCRT + tislelizumab in pMMR/MSS LARC, but confounded by the added PD-1.
digest.tldr: RT-systemic integration is the day's thread across three sites. mRCAT-III doubled pCR in pMMR LARC (61 vs 28.6%) but bundled node-sparing RT with PD-1, so the RT-volume effect is unattributable. Concurrent TRT added nothing to chemoIO in ES-SCLC (HR 1.14). Breast offered a practical traffic-light for timing systemic agents around RT.

sites.intro: A negative randomized test of concurrent thoracic RT layered onto first-line chemoimmunotherapy in ES-SCLC.

studies.name: LBA8005: Concurrent TRT + Chemoimmunotherapy in ES-SCLC
studies.tldr: OS 10.0 vs 11.8mo HR 1.14 (p=0.40): adding concurrent thoracic RT to chemoIO did not improve survival in ES-SCLC.
details: 📊 Adding concurrent thoracic RT to chemoIO improved neither OS nor PFS, with both endpoints numerically favoring chemoIO alone
⚠️ Null even in subgroups predicted to favor TRT: no brain/liver mets, and completers of all 4 chemoIO courses
🔍 Randomized phase III, 1° endpoint OS; treatment-naive ES-SCLC, stage IV or III unfit for curative CRT
📐 N=115 (+TRT) vs 113 (chemoIO alone); stratified by liver and brain metastases

subdetails: Durvalumab 1500mg + carboplatin (AUC5)/etoposide ×4, then durvalumab maintenance until PD
TRT 30 Gy/10 fractions concurrent, starting day 21-28 (~cycle 2), not consolidative
PCI 25-30 Gy to responders; optional WBRT 20-30 Gy for brain mets

details: 🔗 vs CREST (Slotman, Lancet 2015): same 30 Gy/10 fx but consolidative post-chemo, and suggested a thoracic-RT survival benefit
⚠️ Key difference: TRT here is concurrent (day 21-28), not CREST-style consolidation, so doesn't directly refute consolidative TRT
⚠️ Toxicity (a key 2° endpoint) not reported in source; can't weigh added RT harm vs any benefit

verdict.rationale: Randomised phase III with OS primary endpoint; null TRT effect diverges from CREST-era expectation that consolidative thoracic RT benefits ES-SCLC.
verdict.audience: Treatment-naive ES-SCLC, stage IV or III ineligible for curative CRT

open_questions: Does consolidative (sequential) thoracic RT still benefit ES-SCLC in the IO era?
Role of thoracic RT for residual intrathoracic disease after chemoimmunotherapy?

sites.intro: An educational round-up on continuing, holding, or sequencing systemic agents around breast/CW + nodal RT.

studies.name: RT + Systemic Therapy Combinations in Breast Cancer (Speers)
studies.tldr: Educational round-up on continuing, holding, or sequencing systemic agents around breast/chest-wall + nodal RT; PK-based washout the default outside protocols.
details: 🔍 Traffic-light guidance for concurrent systemic therapy with breast/CW + nodal RT (ASCO Educ Book 2026, Table 5)

details.text: 💊 Concurrency with breast/CW + nodal RT, by agent

rows: Continue; dermatitis/pneumonitis vigilance, caution CNS SRS
Caution; sequence/hold for high lung-dose or active pulmonary disease
Usually hold large fields; concurrent only in protocol
Limited fields reasonable; concurrent only in protocol

details.text: 📊 T-DXd ILD signal (the highest-stakes thoracic concurrency)

details: 💊 KEYNOTE-522 post-hoc (1,174 pts, 715 got RT): concurrent pembro+RT well tolerated, numerically fewer G3-5 and immune AEs vs sequential
📊 P-RAD (TBCRC-053): preop RT 0/9/24 Gy + pembro; 24 Gy arm raised T-cell infiltration + TLS at 2 wks (HR+/HER2-)

details.text: ⚠️ PARPi + RT: sequence, don't run concurrent outside trials
subdetails: Veliparib (TBCRC 024): severe acute/late tox concurrent — avoid (moist desquamation, fibrosis)
Olaparib (OlympiA): RT must finish 2-12 wks before; RadioPARP hints concurrent safety
Talazoparib: long t½ (90 hr) complicates brief holds; favors sequential

details: ⚠️ CDK4/6i (abemaciclib/ribociclib/palbociclib): hold for large fields; concurrent only in protocol; evidence mostly retrospective
⚠️ Educational synthesis of largely retrospective + PK data, not new RCT evidence; PK washout → RT → resume is the default

related_trials.brief_title: Radiation Therapy With or Without Olaparib in Treating Patients With Inflammatory Breast Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: This phase II trial studies how well radiation therapy with or without olaparib works in treating patients with inflammatory breast cancer. Radiation therapy uses high energy x-rays to kill tumor cells and shrink tumors. Olaparib is an inhibitor of PARP, an enzyme that helps repair deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) when it becomes damaged. Blocking PARP may help keep cancer cells from repairing their damaged DNA, causing them to die. PARP inhibitors are a type of targeted therapy. It is not yet known…

sites.intro: A node-sparing short-course RT + PD-1 strategy nearly doubled pCR in MSS rectal cancer, though RT volume and IO are confounded.

studies.name: mRCAT-III
studies.tldr: pCR 61.0% vs 28.6% (P<0.0001) adding node-sparing SCRT + tislelizumab vs conventional SCRT+CAPOX in pMMR LARC.
details: 🔍 Phase 3 open-label RCT, 17 sites in China, N=154 (77/arm), stratified by clinical N stage
🔍 Population: pMMR/MSS LARC, cT3-4N0/+M0, tumor ≤10cm from anal verge, no positive lateral nodes
📐 Identical RT dose both arms (short-course 5Gy×5); sole difference is target volume
🔍 Experimental RT spares tumor-draining nodes (tumor bed only) vs conventional elective-nodal coverage
⚠️ Confounded: experimental adds tislelizumab (PD-1) plus node-sparing, control has neither; pCR gain not attributable to RT volume alone
🔗 pMMR/MSS rectal ca: PD-1 usually inactive (unlike dMMR); an IO signal in MSS is the surprising element if it holds
⚠️ pCR is a surrogate; organ preservation, EFS, OS (secondary) unreported, so durability and clinical benefit unknown
🔍 Open-label trial, but pCR/MPR adjudicated by blinded independent central review
Authors report fewer severe GI AEs in the experimental arm; no AE numbers in source

verdict.rationale: Randomized phase 3, primary endpoint via blinded central review, result diverges from MSS-LARC standard. Arms differ by two variables (node-sparing RT + added IO); pCR surrogate, EFS/OS unreported.
verdict.audience: pMMR/MSS LARC, cT3-4N0/+M0, ≤10cm from anal verge

open_questions: Does node-sparing RT specifically (vs adding IO) drive the pCR benefit?
Do pCR gains translate to organ preservation, EFS, OS?
Does the MSS IO signal replicate outside China / single regimen?

related_trials.brief_summary: Most rectal cancers are microsatellite stable (MSS) or mismatch repair-proficient (pMMR) and respond poorly to PD-1 inhibitors. Radiotherapy can enhance tumor antigen release and improve responsiveness to PD-1 blockade in MSS/pMMR rectal cancer. Tumor-draining lymph nodes (TDLNs) are critical sites for anti-tumor immune activation, but radiation-induced damage and fibrosis may impair lymphatic drainage and immune responses. Previous studies have reported a remarkable pathologic complete…

Takeaway Points
• This trial provides evidence that combining node-sparing radiotherapy with
tislelizumab and chemotherapy is an effective treatment strategy for patients
with MMR LARC.
• The experimental arm achieved a remarkable doubling of the pCR rate
(61.0% vs 28.6%, P<0.001), meeting the primary endpoint.
• This innovative approach not only improves tumor response but also reduces
the incidence of severe gastrointestinal adverse events, indicating an
improved tolerability profile.

HER2-Directed Therapy + Radiation
The mAbs are settled - the ADC question is T-DM1 vs T-DXd
13
V Trastuzumab + Pertuzumab - concurrent
with breast/CW + nodal RT is standard practice
Acceptable toxicity, No serious adverse events with concurrent treatment
HERA trial - trastuzumab initiated after completion of chemotherapy and XRT
Finter trial ART was started after compretion of chemotherapy and rastuzumab
NCCTG N9831 - XRT given concurrently with trastuzumab
APHINITY Trial - trastuzumab and pertuzumab given concurrently with RT
T-DM 1 ado-rastuzumab emtansine; t/½ = 4 days, 5t½ = 20 days
CONTINUE during locoregional RT as on
KATHERINE or ATEMPT Trials
KEY TOXICITY SIGNALS
Dermatitis may be underreported (Corbin)
Pneumonitis small signal - maintain vigilance
CNS radionecrosis with SRS - the "do not ignore" signal
USE CAUTION WITH
large lung volumes • IMN coverage • bolus • reconstruction •
CNS SRS
T-DX trastuzumab deruxtecan; t/½ = 6 days, 5t% = 30 days
REASONABLE with caution - monitor closely
DOMINANT TOXICITY - ILD
PI (5.4 mg/kg): ILD ~12%; fatal ~ 0.9%
DESTINY-Breast05: ILD 9.6% T-DXd vs 1.6% T-DM1
RT timing (T-Dd arm): 10.7% seq vs 9.6% concurrent - no effect
COMBART: 40 pts concurrent RT/SRT; acute tox 20%
SEQUENCE OR HOLD FOR
high lung-dose plans • baseline ILD • thoracic/lung RT •
symptomatic pulmonary disease

CLINICAL
ONCOLOGY
ASCO• KNOWLEDGE CONQUERS
CANCER

15
PARPi, Capecitabine, and CDK4/6i + Radiation
Where caution is non-negotiable - and where to hold
Olaparib
½ = 15 hr, 5t½ = 3 days
OlympiA, RadioPARP, S1706, OlympiAD
RT must complete 2-12 wks BEFORE olaparib
Sequential paradigm in early-stage;
RadioPARP suggests concurrent safety
Tutt et al., NEJM 2021;384:2394-2405
Loap et al., JROBP, 2025 Nov 1: 123(3):726-731
Veliparib
½ = 5.5 hr, 5t½ = 1 day
TBCRC 024
Severe acute + late toxicity with concurrent RT
AVOID concurrency - dose-limiting moist
desquamation, fibrosis
Jagsi et al., J Clin Oncol 2018; 36(13): 1317-1322
Talazoparib
t½ = 90 hr; 5t½ = 19 days
PK tail
Long t½ complicates brief holds
Favors sequential strategy in early-stage
Capecitabine
t½ = ~45 min; 5t½ = 4 hrs
CREATE-X. Phase I/ Concurrent Trial Woodward et al.
Adjuvant paradigm is sequential, not concurrent
AVOID concurrent breast/CW RT outside
protocol; resume after RT
Masuda et al. N Engl J Med. 2017;376(22):2147-2159.
Woodward et al. JROBP. 2017;99(4):777-783.
Abemaciclib / Ribociclib
t½ = 24-55 hr; 5t = 5-11 days
NCT05996107
Limited prospective data; most evidence
retrospective
Hold during RT for large fields;
concurrent feasible in trial setting
Palbociclib
t½ = 28.8 hr 5t½ = 6 days
5 t½ = 6 days
Marrow / GI exposure increases with field size
Practical to hold around RT; resume
promptly after
When in doubt, hold. PK-based washout → RT → resume — the safer default outside protocols

CLINICAL ONCOLOGY
ASCO• KNOWLEDGE CONQUERS

16
Putting It Together: A Traffic-Light Summary
Concurrent systemic therapy with breast / chest wall + RNI
V CONTINUE
Concurrent with RT
Endocrine therapy
Minimal radiosensitization
Trastuzumab ‡ Pertuzumab
Generally safe; modern heart-sparing
! CAUTION
Plan- / setting-dependent
T-DXd
Concurrent reasonable with monitoring;
sequence/hold for high lung-dose or active
pulmonary disease
CDK4/6 inhibitors
Usually hold for large fields; concurrent only in
selected/protocol settings
X HOLD / SEQUENCE
Outside dedicated protocol
Cytotoxic chemotherapy
Anthracyclines, taxanes, platinum -
sequence, do not give concurrently
Capecitabine / PARPI
(veliparib/talazoparib)
Adjuvant paradigm non-concurrent, veliparib +
RT shows severe acute/late tox
T-DM1
Continue during locoregional RT; dermatitis &
pneumonitis vigilance; caution with CNS SRS
Immunotherapy (Pembro)
Generally tolerable; pneumonitis vigilance +
immune-toxicity workflows
Olaparib
Reasonable with limited fields; concurrent only
in selected/protocol settings
mTOR / PISK agents
Mucosal, skin, metabolic, inflammatory toxicity
risks; ESMO-ESTRO advises caution
